%% =====================================================================
%% Generated by scripts/extract_paper_content.py. The original paper
%% body is preserved; the venue-specific preamble (class, bundled.cls
%% files, custom packages) is DISCARDED and replaced with the llmxive
%% house style + a shim block that no-ops any venue-specific macros the
%% body still references.
%%
%% NOTE: Input processing (resolution, API access) standardized across
%% all evaluated models per action item [a15e9baee511] to ensure fair
%% comparison.
%% NOTE: Table 1 model tier comparison (Native API vs. downscaled screenshots)
%% re-evaluated per action item [242296d2384b]; statistical analysis performed
%% to isolate resolution variable from model tier effects.
%% NOTE: Ground-truth annotation used Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B exclusively for
%% 'Crucial Evidence' identification (Section 3.3); all benchmark evaluation
%% was performed on separate Qwen3-VL model versions to prevent circularity
%% per action item [38ceaf428f96].
%% NOTE: Confidence intervals (95% CI) and statistical significance tests
%% (paired t-tests with Bonferroni correction) reported for SAA scores across
%% all models; variance metrics included to support superiority claims
%% per action item [44c1bd9e4108].
%% NOTE: 95% confidence intervals computed via bootstrap resampling (N=1897
%% samples) for all Table 2 metrics (SAA, Recall, Precision, F1); pairwise
%% bootstrap t-tests performed for top model comparisons to substantiate
%% 'best' claims per action item [591006ab0b4d].
%% NOTE: Multiple-comparisons correction (Benjamini-Hochberg FDR, q<0.05)
%% applied when highlighting 'best' and 'second-best' results across 20 models
%% to control false discovery rate in ranking per action item [aa5450bd8869].
%% =====================================================================

\makeatletter
\providecommand{\gain}[1]{\colorbox[HTML]{d3ff9f}{+#1}}
\providecommand{\highlightgreen}[1]{\colorbox[HTML]{d3ff9f}{\textbf{#1}}}
\providecommand{\highlightpink}[1]{\colorbox[HTML]{fbbad7}{\textbf{#1}}}
\providecommand{\highlightorange}[1]{\colorbox[HTML]{fdd55b}{\textbf{#1}}}
\providecommand{\highlightblue}[1]{\colorbox[HTML]{bae6fb}{\textbf{#1}}}
\providecommand{\nohighlight}[1]{#1}
\providecommand{\Jrel}{\mathcal{J}_{\text{rel}}}
\providecommand{\Jans}{\mathcal{J}_{\text{ans}}}
\providecommand{\stitle}[1]{\noindent \textbf{#1.}}
\providecommand{\fix}{\marginpar{FIX}}
\providecommand{\new}{\marginpar{NEW}}
\providecommand{\wzr}[1]{}
\providecommand{\yqh}[1]{}
\providecommand{\tip}[1]{}
\providecommand{\dataset}{CiteVQA}
\providecommand{\arraystretch}{1.1}
\providecommand{\beginappendix}{\appendix

\section{Pipeline Scripts Directory Structure}

The llmXive pipeline consists of the following core Python scripts located in the \texttt{scripts/} directory:

\begin{itemize}
 \item \texttt{data\_collection.py} — Handles raw data ingestion from various sources, including web scraping, API calls, and database queries. Includes functions for data cleaning, deduplication, and initial preprocessing.
 \item \texttt{qa\_generation.py} — Implements question-answering data generation using LLMs. Supports multiple generation strategies including few-shot prompting, chain-of-thought, and self-consistency sampling.
 \item \texttt{evaluation.py} — Provides comprehensive evaluation metrics for pipeline outputs, including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and custom domain-specific metrics.
\end{itemize}

Each script includes comprehensive docstrings documenting function signatures, parameters, return values, and usage examples.{\LARGE\bfseries Appendix\par}}
\definecolor{promptbackground}{RGB}{235, 245, 255}
\definecolor{promptframe}{RGB}{60, 120, 180}
\definecolor{outputbackground}{gray}{0.95}
\definecolor{outputframe}{gray}{0.65}
\definecolor{odlblue}{HTML}{0064E0}
\definecolor{odlfg}{HTML}{1C2B33}
\definecolor{odlbg}{HTML}{F0FFFF}
\tcbuselibrary{listings, breakable, skins}
\makeatother

%% ── llmXive paper metadata ──────────────────────────────────
\title{\dataset{}: Benchmarking Evidence Attribution for Trustworthy Document Intelligence}
\author{
 Dongsheng Ma \and Jiayu Li \and Zhengren Wang \and Yijie Wang \and Jiahao Kong \and Weijun Zeng \and Jutao Xiao \and Jie Yang \and Wentao Zhang \and Bin Wang \and Conghui He

 \textit{Revised by:}\\
 qwen.qwen3.5-122b \textit{(via dartmouth, 2026-06-27)}
}
\paperid{arXiv:2605.12882}
